This toolkit not definitive list technology resources, nor designed address non-technical issues, such attendance policies pedagogical best practices.
You will not need take action every the toolkit. Simply pick and choose the information that most useful for your teaching context. part your preparations, protect your remote location (home) computers with anti-virus software help ensure they will reliable when you need them.

Modern smart phones and cell connected devices provide the best way access email during emergencies. Cell networks are often buffered from the disruptions power and are less susceptible wind damage than cable, phone and fiber networks. When the worst does happen cellular services are some the first systems restored. Microsoft Outlook for IOS and Android provides modern interface with functionality superior either Suite Gmail Office 365 Exchange Online. Inbox Google also provides updated user experience compared native phone apps.
Request email distribution list for your class MyUW under the Teaching tab. These class lists are created the time they are requested and will ready use the following day. you want create list for cross-listed courses combinations courses, submit Mailman Instructor Class List request. You could also post the class contact list connect students with one another.
Most social networking services like SnapChat, Twitter and Facebook have wide adoption, but are not safe for FERPA and HIPAA content. These services may used for some purposes, but great care would need taken not expose sensitive information, such who course. Consider using Yammer, which provides FERPA and HIPAA-aligned environment.
Skype for Business, which both HIPAA– and FERPA-aligned, allows you conduct chat with participants and meeting with 250 participants. Learn more here.
Zoom makes easy for faculty, staff, and students participate group video chat during online class sessions, host virtual office hours, collaborate remotely research, share screens and host real-time video conversations, host live web broadcasts thousands people worldwide and record the cloud computer for easy sharing. For more information Zoom free accounts and Zoom Pro accounts and differences between the two learn more here.
Google Hangouts works with people time. While you can invite many people you want join hangout, you can’t have more than people hangout once, while others will get stuck hanging lobby. Hangouts FERPA–aligned, but does not have the BAA required HIPAA. Hangouts feature Suite. For more about how Hangouts works, see Google’s page about Google Hangouts. Both Google Hangouts and Skype for Business are good tools for chats and conferencing.
Turn your voicemail box into telephone hotline. Simply update your greeting with timely information about the status your course(s) campus operations. Contact Information Technology (help@uw.edu) create enhanced hotline functionality such call menus.
The Canvas Learning Management system offers conference feature, available every course, that you can use meet live with students. Conferences can accommodate people. Learn more.
You can also use Zoom Skype for Business. Zoom unifies cloud video conferencing, simple online meetings, group messaging, and conference room solution into one easy-to-use platform. For more information Zoom you can learn more here. Skype for Business (formerly known “Lync”), which both HIPAA– and FERPA-aligned, allows you conduct chat with participants and meeting with 250 participants.
Panopto cloud-based lecture capture service. Panopto lets instructors record and publish lectures and other class activities online. Students can then view (and review) the published materials using Web browser mobile device. You can use Panopto two modes: pre-record and then post video lectures, broadcast live lecture (with three-second delay). The first mode allows you upload supplemental video content and add notes your course materials. Panopto not meant used conferencing tool. find out more about Panopto, visit the Panopto Help Center.
Canvas Student Groups are like smaller version your course and are used collaborative tool where students can work together group projects and assignments.
Every Canvas course includes the ability create and review quizzes. Learn more about creating and managing quizzes Canvas. Learn more about creating quizzes Canvas.
The Canvas Gradebook helps instructors easily record student scores online, track student progress and calculate final grades. Grades for each assignment can calculated points, percentages, complete incomplete, pass fail, GPA scale, and letter grades. Provide secure access and TAs view and edit student grades. Publish scores for students view securely online.
GradePage simple interface for quarterly grade submission, can used GradeBook users, Canvas users and those who manage their grades using other software traditional approach.
Methods are available for accessing campus materials from your personal laptop home computer. First check with your departmental support staff; your department may already have system place for accessing files from home.
Most faculty connect the network from home through their Internet Service Provider (ISP). Connecting the Network will allow you access most your class resources and tools from home the event that you need conduct your class remotely.
The preferred method connecting through Husky OnNet (based the BIG-IP platform) which provides individual users with secure temporary connection the network from remote locations. Husky OnNet for MacOS and Husky OnNet for Windows are available for download from UWare. Android, iOS,and other BIG-IP apps are available from third-party app stores.
Remote desktop access allows you access your work computer using another computer with connection the Internet. Your icons, programs and printers are all available. You can even transfer files between your systems. (For security purposes, remote desktop should only used conjunction with secure connection the network such Husky OnNet.) order use remote desktop access, your work computer will need remain (you can logged out, but the computer must not turned off) and you will need know your work computer’s current address name. you need help finding your address, contact your local support staff. Find out more about this option Connecting Remotely.
Because remote desktop access requires your work computer remain when you are not work, the better option store your materials online (see “Store Your Course Materials Online” section, below) and then use Husky OnNet (see “Connect the Networks” section, below).
edu) regarding telephones, NetIDs, MyUW, email distribution lists, and general help with any the technologies mentioned above.
Contact the Center for Teaching and Learning (thectl@uw.edu) talk about best practices, whether out the classroom.
Contact Disability Services Office (dso@uw.edu) ensure all your students with disabilities are accommodated your technology choices.
For information about campus pandemic planning and during possible pandemic: Emergency Management Website, MyUW emergency announcements, UWTV, email.

original text:
About Shape What and How Got Here.
Chris the CEO Shape. responsible for architecting and developing Shape’s core platform. the past, has taken lead roles large scale software projects for marketing agencies and other tech companies like Intel. Chris brings that exceptional experience and technical skill, not mention his great attitude, the Shape team.
David co-founder and Senior Developer. talented full stack coder responsible for maintaining the site and developing new features. attended Oregon State University pursuing degree Computer Science before jumping into the startup world full time. When isn't working Shape enjoys hanging out with his girlfriend and rooting for his favorite athlete Novak Djokovic.
Dominic the Engineering Shape. joined the founders through series very fortunate encounters Bend, Oregon. His focus constantly shifts wherever the fire hottest. commands the front end the Shape platform, directs engineering resources the back end, and provides design work for the sales and marketing team. Outside work, loves explore the world's places with good friends and family.
Jon co-founder Shape. spent years PPC consultant, agency analyst and department manager before leaving build useful apps for other PPC analysts. The origins Shape come from problems Jon saw first hand the industry. focuses product direction, customer service and sales for our company, reach out him with any questions jon@shape.io.
Maxwel the 'jack all trades' the Shape office working Sales Development, Customer Service, Client Success, Digital Marketing, and Culture Appreciation. graduate Oregon State University living Bend, Oregon, Max brings load digital marketing and sales experience the team. not the Shape office, Max likes long rides Mt. Bachelor, good game his hands, and time well spent with those cares about.
Nicole the Marketing Shape. She has extensive background the PPC industry working analyst, department head and product manager. Nicole's focus crafting our website, marketing materials and support documents clearly communicate how PPC professionals can benefit from our platform. When she's not working, she enjoys hiking with her husband and half golden tornado (Parker), baking, and reading.
Tyler Business Development Shape. comes from years analyst, account manager and sales consultant focused the PPC industry. was responsible for millions dollars client spend his agency job Shape customer. Tyler excited use his experience help relate the challenges that face PPC analysts and how Shape can help. When isn't working enjoys golfing and playing with his dog Carl.
Our founding team's experience digital marketing lends perspective product decisions that especially empathetic the day-to-day challenges faced our customers. This the product always wished existed.
We've spent lot time manually updating Excel spreadsheets and Google Docs. analyst's time valuable. Time should spent the fun challenges being analyst: the optimization leading improvement performance. Analysts know the more time spent optimizing the better for everyone.
Shape team developers, designers and analysts with the goal serving digital marketing professionals and the organizations they run.
love hearing ideas from analysts about how software could make their lives better. you have idea for improvement, suggestion for Shape, please reach out. View our Contact Page for more details about how get touch.

Man Fut Tong Nursing Home has range volunteer opportunities for individual group involvement. Volunteers play important role meeting the specific needs our elderly residents and Day Day Care Centre’s beneficiaries. All volunteers have unique role play helping our elderly succeed and function with dignity and pride. Our current volunteers come from CCs, Army HQ, Schools, Welfare organizations and individuals.
you are seriously interested giving your time and skills that you have help elderly, please contact Rachel Denise 3157 1158 email vm@mft.org.sg indicating “volunteer” the subject line.

The minutes IAB meetings 2007-09-05 and 2007-09-19 were approved. Approval the minutes the IAB meeting 2007-10-03 were deferred until the next business meeting.
Oct the IESG sent email the community re-affirming its reading RFC 2026 regarding appeals.
There has been lot discussion about upcoming BoFs for the Vancouver meeting, please see and remember that there joint meeting October 25.
Sept 24, Stewart Bryant sent “Report ITU-T Q12/15 Meeting Stuttgart September 2007” the IESG and IAB. addition, there have been discussions between the ADs and other IETF members present this meeting and leaders and members the ITU-T press release messages and the like coming out it.
has been undergoing review and update. scheduled for second round IESG telechat discussion (and possible approval) Oct 18.
“Study Groups” Experiment has been discussed length. final decision has been made, but can report that looks like experiment approved will very limited scope.
The IESG appointed Simon Josefsson the designated IANA expert for RFC 4422.
The IESG has been discussing and Cullen Jennings has drafted IESG Statement regarding the deadline for version Working Group documents.
The IETF hosted mailing lists have had some problems with some users’ autoresponse (“out the office”) messages. Chris Newman will draft policy Autoresponse Messages sent IETF hosted mailing lists.
IRTF report was received.
beta version the online errata submission form has been made available the IAB, IESG, and IRSG, well handful individuals that have provided number errata the past. Beta login and verification screens are follow shortly.
was posted August 2007. Some comments replies are the rfc-interest list.
met with IANA and Bill Fenner discuss future integration data between the IANA, IESG Secretariat, and RFC Editor. will meet again Vancouver.
Danny noted that there had not yet been opinion voiced the IAB.
you know ISOC held Board Strategic Retreat early October Trust. Olaf has sent memo wrt IAB possible items and “report” will published ISOC due course (and sent the IETF leadership). Thanks Russ, Olaf and Danny for their support and participation.
have included below copy invitation meeting being hosted ISOC wrt v4/v6. Report out meeting follow (late October).
was also noted that there would round table Amsterdam Monday October the RIPE NCC offices between the five RIRs and the IETF leadership.
The IETF was approached from ISO/IEC JTC1, with request become Approved Reference Organisation (ARO) ISO/IEC JTC1. decided act upon this request positively. Action Olaf return the form.
Loa provided brief review the need establish IETF design team consider what work might required pursue development T-MPLS within the IETF. was noted that this design team was intended carry out preparatory work for the IETF, and was separate from any future group ITU and IETF participants which might collaborate design work.
was decided that the IAB should send request for participation the IESG, the ietf@ietf.org list and appropriate IETF working group lists. Loa draft text.
was agreed unanimously appoint Monique Morrow the role IETF liaison ITU-NGN. Dave Oran follow with Monique specific details the duties and role IETF liaison manager..
Various possible topics for presentation the Technical Plenary Vancouver were discussed. Further review the proposals received necessary. decision was reached.
Dave Oran alerted the theIAB ongoing discussions with the SIP Forum for them host bar-BoF activity the next IETF meeting engage the community discussion how improve among SIP The BoF will likely take the form lunchtime workshop where people will able present ideas and proposals for where needs improved, and how.
The IAB entered executive session discuss the IETF’s appointee the ICANN NomCom.

original text:
know, know it’s Saturday. But yesterday was busy, and then was sunny walked the pub after work. And this morning I’ve been upgrading Windows and reading offline.
Canva love this for quickly pulling together photos I’ve taken, for creating mash ups for the Daily Create. made the image the top this post with Canva using some photos that took yesterday.
Pullquote neat little app for Chrome etc. use this lot quickly tweet things that are longer than the 140 character limit.
Hypothes.is love this annotation tool especially for adding gifs and images the side blog posts.
Plant.net Another app use lot when I’m out walking around the Scottish Highlands and Islands. Great for identifying unknown plants and confirming when unsure.
Pocket often when come across something want read, want save read later. This another Chrome extension that use lot save stuff for when have time read it.
That’s five regular “go to” apps online. What are your favourites?
This entry was posted #CLMOOC, Garden, Online learning, Photos, Scotland, Technology and tagged #clmooc, Canva, Pl@nt.net, pocket, pullquote.is. Bookmark the permalink.

original text:
The Strokes‘ long-awaited comeback single ‘Juice Box’ has leaked online.
The track, the first lifted from their third album ‘First Impressions Earth’, isn’t officially due for release until early next year.
However, fans are swapping MP3 files the guitar-heavy track online.
The single shows change direction for the band, with Julian Casablancas singing “Why won’t you come over here we’ve got city love” during the chorus.
Have you heard the song? What you think it? Email your comments news@nme.com now. Also, next week NME (out September 28) you’ll find the world exclusive comeback interview with the band.
processed_text:
The Strokes‘ long-awaited comeback single ‘Juice Box’ has leaked online.
The track, the first lifted from their third album ‘First Impressions Earth’, isn’t officially due for release until early next year.
However, fans are swapping MP3 files the guitar-heavy track online.
The single shows change direction for the band, with Julian Casablancas singing “Why won’t you come over here we’ve got city love” during the chorus.
Have you heard the song? What you think it? Email your comments  now. Also, next week NME (out September 28) you’ll find the world exclusive comeback interview with the band.
